title: Doctor, Then Pharmacy
description: You visit the doctor, get a prescription, and find your way to the pharmacy.
context: You go to the doctor because you don't feel well. After the appointment, you find the pharmacy and pick up your medicine.

exchanges.speaker: doctor
exchanges.prompt: The doctor asks what's wrong. Pick whichever symptom fits.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Correct pattern — Bolí ma + body part, or Mám + symptom. Any of these works to describe how you feel.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Bolí ma + body part or Mám + symptom is the pattern. Pick whichever describes you.

exchanges.speaker: doctor
exchanges.translation: I understand. How long has this been going on?
exchanges.prompt: The doctor asks how long. It started yesterday. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Od včera. — correct. Od + time tells the doctor exactly when the symptoms began.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Od včera (since yesterday) is correct here. Od + time period is the pattern for saying since when.

exchanges.speaker: doctor
exchanges.text: Dobre, dám vám predpis. Berte tieto tabletky dvakrát denne.
exchanges.translation: Good, I'll give you a prescription. Take these pills twice a day.
exchanges.prompt: The doctor explains the dosage. How do you confirm you understood?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Rozumiem, ďakujem. — correct. You confirmed understanding and thanked the doctor.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Rozumiem, ďakujem (I understand, thank you) is the right response when you've followed the instructions.

exchanges.speaker: doctor
exchanges.text: Lekáreň je blízko. Choďte rovno z budovy a je hneď vedľa banky.
exchanges.translation: The pharmacy is close. Go straight out of the building and it's right next to the bank.
exchanges.prompt: The doctor gives directions. Where is the pharmacy located relative to the bank?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Vedľa banky. — correct. Vedľa = next to, from the Positions lesson, exactly where the doctor said the pharmacy is.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Vedľa banky (next to the bank) matches what the doctor said — listen carefully to position words.

exchanges.speaker: doctor
exchanges.prompt: The doctor says goodbye warmly. How do you respond?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Dovidenia, ďakujem veľmi pekne! — correct, formal and warm goodbye.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Dovidenia, ďakujem veľmi pekne! is the formal, warm way to say goodbye to a doctor.

exchanges.speaker: pharmacist
exchanges.text: Dobrý deň. Ako vám môžem pomôcť?
exchanges.translation: Good day. How can I help you?
exchanges.prompt: You found the pharmacy. You have a prescription. What do you say?

choices.translation: I have a prescription from the doctor.
Do you have something for a cold?

exchanges.correctFeedback: Mám predpis od doktora. — correct. The pharmacist now knows to process your prescription.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Mám predpis od doktora (I have a prescription from the doctor) is the right way to start when you have one.

exchanges.speaker: pharmacist
exchanges.text: Dobre, tu sú vaše tabletky. Viete ako ich užívať?
exchanges.translation: Good, here are your pills. Do you know how to take them?
exchanges.prompt: The pharmacist hands you the pills and asks if you know the instructions. You want to confirm. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Ako to mám užívať? — correct. Asking this confirms you'll take the medicine correctly.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Ako to mám užívať? (How should I take it?) is the right question to confirm the instructions.

exchanges.speaker: pharmacist
exchanges.prompt: The pharmacist gives the instructions. What do you say to confirm you understood?

choices.translation: Twice a day, before meals. I understand.
Do you have something for a headache?

exchanges.correctFeedback: Dvakrát denne, pred jedlom. Rozumiem. — perfect, you repeated it back to confirm.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Repeating the instructions back — Dvakrát denne, pred jedlom. Rozumiem — confirms you understood correctly.

exchanges.speaker: pharmacist
exchanges.translation: Excellent. Do you need anything else?
exchanges.prompt: You also want a plaster for a cut finger. What do you ask?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Áno, potrebujem náplasť. — correct. Náplasť = plaster, exactly what you need for your finger.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Áno, potrebujem náplasť (Yes, I need a plaster) is correct since you also need that.

exchanges.speaker: pharmacist
exchanges.text: Tu máte. Dovidenia a uzdravte sa!
exchanges.translation: Here you go. Goodbye and get well soon!
exchanges.prompt: The pharmacist hands you the plaster and says goodbye. How do you respond?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Ďakujem veľmi pekne, dovidenia! — warm and complete closing to the whole visit.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Ďakujem veľmi pekne, dovidenia! (Thank you very much, goodbye!) is the warm way to close out your pharmacy visit.

completionMessage: 🎉 Great job! You described your symptoms, understood the doctor's directions to the pharmacy, confirmed your medicine instructions, and got everything you needed — all in Slovak.
